entitySuffixRule: Must contain the words "limited liability company" or the abbreviation "L.L.C." or "LLC." The word "Limited" may be abbreviated "Ltd." and "company" may be abbreviated "Co."
distinguishabilityNote: Must be distinguishable on the records of the Secretary of the State from the name of any entity formed by a state filing that is not administratively dissolved, any foreign entity registered to do business in Connecticut, any name reserved under § 34-243l, and any name registered under § 34-243m. Words and abbreviations indicating a type of entity (corporation, Inc., Ltd., LP, LLP, and the like) are not taken into account. A non-distinguishable name may be used only with a court judgment establishing the company's right to it.
nameReservationCostBasis: The $60 Secretary of the State fee (Application for Reservation of Name) is the full cost. It is among the higher reservation fees in the country.
notes: Connecticut places LLC naming in its Uniform Limited Liability Company Act, so entity types share one distinguishability register at the Secretary of the State. Section 34-243k lists no banned words but bars language implying a purpose the LLC is not organized for; "bank," "trust," and "insurance" are cleared through the relevant regulators under other titles. The $60 reservation is one of the priciest holds in this dataset, and the only route past a conflicting name is a court judgment rather than a simple consent letter. Connecticut is administered by the Secretary of the State's Business Services Division.
